methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Stark Defence
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

private.name: Vatn Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $300M

private.name: Ares Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
Rune Technologies is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by David Tuttle and Peter Goldsborough that has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $20M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier to next-stage transactions, yields $40M. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables (Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M) produces a median valuation of $300M, with a range of $150M to $500M.

The two methods exhibit material divergence. The triangulated valuation range is $34M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $170M. This wide spread reflects two distinct valuation signals: a capital-efficiency view implied by stage progression, and a market-comparable view anchored to peer transactions. Key uncertainties include whether Rune's technology, team, or traction warrant premium positioning within the peer cohort, and whether the peer set adequately reflects market conditions at time of Series A close. Further diligence on revenue, customer concentration, and IP strength is warranted to narrow the range.
